meta.title: D150 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Land Rover Evoque D150 MHEV — real UK costs, EGR & DPF insight, and honest reliability data for the 2.0 Ingenium diesel. Get free quotes from 12 vetted Land Rover specialists today.

@graph.name: Land Rover Evoque D150 Engine Era Map
Land Rover Evoque D150 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Evoque D150 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned D150 2.0-litre Ingenium diesel engine typically costs £3,500–£5,800 supply only. Used engines are available from £2,000–£3,500. Fitting adds £1,300–£2,000 depending on your location and workshop. Most Evoque engine replacements range between £2,500 and £7,500.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Evoque D150 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The D150 is powered by the post-2019 revised Ingenium diesel, which addressed the early timing chain tensioner failures seen on pre-2019 units. However, it still has known issues: EGR cooler leaks, DPF clogging (urban use), and occasional timing chain tensioner wear. The 48V MHEV system adds complexity but is generally reliable. Regular maintenance (12,000-mile oil changes with JLR-spec oil) is essential.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Evoque D150?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are EGR cooler leaks, DPF clogging (short urban journeys), and timing chain tensioner wear (reduced risk on post-2019 but still possible). The MHEV system's battery and electronics are generally reliable but add complexity.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an Evoque D150 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £28,000 Evoque, a £4,500 recon is 16% of the car's value — definitely worth it. Against a high-mileage £11,000 example, the same recon approaches our 60% Land Rover threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Evoque D150 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The D150 carries the engine code 204DTD (also referred to as AJ20D4 or Ingenium D150 in some documentation). It's a 2.0-litre four-cylinder diesel with a 48V mild-hybrid system, used in the Evoque L551 from 2019 onwards.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain tensioners, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative timing chain tensioner replacement costs £800–£1,500. If ignored and the chain skips, timing is lost, valves contact pistons, and you're looking at a £3,500–£5,800 engine replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Regular oil changes are the single most effective preventative measure for the revised 2019+ tensioners.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Evoque D150 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the 204DTD MHEV Ingenium diesel. Most Evoque engine replacements range between £2,500 and £7,500 depending on engine type and specification.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an Evoque D150 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £70–£100/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

cta.label: → Compare D150 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: EGR Cooler Leaks
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white exhaust smoke, occasional burning smell, engine management light.
problems.recommendation: EGR cooler failure is a well-documented issue on the Ingenium diesel family, including the MHEV version. If you're experiencing unexplained coolant loss, have the EGR cooler inspected promptly. Ignoring it can lead to overheating and potential engine damage, though it doesn't typically progress to full engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full EGR cooler failure guide

problems.title: DPF Clogging & Regeneration Issues
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, reduced performance, increased fuel consumption, failed regeneration cycles.
problems.recommendation: The Ingenium diesel's DPF is sensitive to short urban journeys. The MHEV system helps reduce engine load at low speeds but doesn't eliminate DPF clogging risk. If you predominantly drive in town, ensure the vehicle completes regular regeneration cycles — a motorway run at 2,000+ rpm for 20 minutes at least once a week is recommended.

failureLink.label: Read the full DPF failure guide

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear (Post-2019 — Reduced Risk)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start lasting 1–2 seconds, metallic ticking from the engine.
problems.replacementCost: £3,500–£5,800 (if chain skips and valves contact pistons)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle lasting more than 2 seconds, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: Land Rover revised the timing chain tensioner design in 2019, significantly reducing the failure rate seen on earlier Ingenium diesels. However, tensioner wear remains a possibility, especially if oil changes are neglected or the wrong oil specification is used. At the first sign of cold-start rattle lasting more than 2 seconds, get the chain and tensioners inspected immediately.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Evoque D150 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Forced regeneration or DPF clean — less than replacement
Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Typically requires full engine replacement or complete rebuild

buyingChecks.title: Buying This D150? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2019 build (revised timing chain tensioners fitted from factory)
* Documented service history with 0W-30 or 5W-30 JLR-spec oil at 12,000-mile (or less) intervals
* Evidence of DPF-friendly driving — regular motorway runs
* No cold-start rattle — a quiet startup suggests healthy tensioners
avoidIf: * Pre-2019 build (unless timing chain tensioners have been replaced with revised parts)
* No proof of regular oil changes — the Ingenium diesel is unforgiving of neglect
* DPF warning light with no regeneration history
* Engine warning light with EGR or DPF codes and no diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The D150 is the entry-point diesel for the L551 Evoque, and the 2019 revisions made the Ingenium diesel meaningfully more reliable than its predecessor. But it's still a modern diesel with modern diesel problems — EGR coolers, DPFs, and timing chain tensioners all need attention. Buy on documented history, not on faith. A well-maintained D150 is a £25,000–£35,000 luxury compact SUV; a neglected one is a £4,000 engine replacement waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare D150 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Evoque D150 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or metallic ticking on startup (timing chain tensioner wear warning — post-2019 reduced but not eliminated)
* Service History — including the D150 MHEV era (2019–present) and confirmed oil-change intervals using JLR-spec 0W-30 or 5W-30 oil
* DPF Check — Check for DPF warning light or evidence of regeneration issues (urban-driven vehicles are high risk)
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR coolant leak
* Coolant Level — Any history of coolant loss without visible leaks? (EGR cooler warning)
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved EGR, DPF, or timing-related issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (DPF, EGR, or injector-related)
